meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve can't debug code
content.seo_description: Learn how to use Git with AI to overcome debugging roadblocks. A beginner-level checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first app.
content.answer_block: Debugging is the primary hurdle for every student. By integrating Git version control with AI tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you transform error logs into actionable intelligence. This workflow automates root-cause analysis, tracks structural changes, and provides a safety net to revert breaking changes instantly while building your portfolio.
content.intro:
The screen glows with a sea of red error text, a silent testament to the hours you've poured into your first project. That sinking feeling of being stuck—the 'can't debug code' paralysis—is not a failure; it is the exact moment where a student becomes an engineer. You are standing at the threshold of mastery.

Today, we dismantle that barrier. By marrying the precision of Git version control with the analytical power of AI, you will stop guessing and start architecting. This is not just about fixing a bug; it is about building a professional-grade feedback loop that will accelerate your growth and make your code production-ready.

steps.title: Initialize Git for Contextual Awareness
steps.description: Before AI can help, it needs a history. Initialize a repository to create a snapshot of your current stable state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project structure and explain the core dependencies required for this feature.
steps.claude_code_command: git init && git add . && git commit -m 'Initial project structure'

steps.title: Capture the Error State
steps.description: Don't just fix the code; document the error. Use Git to branch off before you attempt a risky fix.
steps.antigravity_prompt: I am receiving the following error: [Paste Error]. Analyze the stack trace and identify the likely module causing this.

steps.title: AI-Driven Root Cause Analysis
steps.description: Use Claude Code to parse your logs and compare them against your current codebase to find the logic gap.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Compare my recent commits to the current error state. Which line of code introduced the regression?
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze the last 3 commits and locate the regression causing the current error'

steps.title: Implement and Verify the Fix
steps.description: Apply the fix suggested by the AI and verify it against your test suite.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this function to handle the edge case identified in the error log while maintaining readability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Apply the refactor, then run the test suite to verify the fix'

steps.title: Commit with Contextual Documentation
steps.description: Finalize your fix by committing with a message that explains the 'why' behind the change.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize the bug and the fix in a professional commit message format.
steps.claude_code_command: git add . && git commit -m 'Fix: Resolved [Bug Name] by refactoring [Module]'

pro_tips: Always commit before asking the AI to refactor; it makes 'undoing' a bad suggestion instantaneous.
Use 'git diff' before committing to see exactly what the AI changed—never blindly accept code.
Tag your commits with 'AI-Fix' to keep track of how many bugs your AI assistant helped you resolve.
When stuck, ask the AI to 'explain like I'm a junior engineer' to bridge gaps in your conceptual knowledge.
Keep your commits atomic—one bug fix per commit makes it easier to track progress in your portfolio.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully debugged your first module, move to implementing a CI/CD pipeline to automate these tests, ensuring your code stays clean as your project scales.
content.conversion_block: Stop struggling with cryptic error messages and start building with the confidence of a lead developer. You have the tools, the workflow, and the roadmap. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
